page: Vultr voc-s-8c-64gb-1280s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server powered by an AMD EPYC x86_64 processor. It features 8 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core under a shared CPU allocation model. The processor operates at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz and includes 16 MB of L3 cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 KB of L1d cache. It supports key instruction sets such as AVX, AES, SHA_NI, BMI2, and FMA, though it lacks AVX-512 extensions.

The server is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Storage is provided by a bundled 1280 GB local NVMe SSD, offering high-capacity local storage for data-intensive operations. In benchmark evaluations, the system demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% via stress-ng, while multi-core CPU performance falls within the average range. Memory performance is also strong, with PassMark memory scores placing in the top 25%, supported by average memory read and write bandwidths.

Tradeoffs include a shared CPU allocation model, which may introduce performance variability under sustained high-compute workloads compared to dedicated instances. This is reflected in average multi-core processing and database operation benchmarks, as well as weak performance in medium-model LLM prompt processing. However, the high memory density and large NVMe storage capacity offer qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring significant memory and fast local storage without the premium of dedicated CPU cores. Appropriate workloads include memory-intensive applications, caching layers, database hosting, and general-purpose web serving.
description: Vultr voc-s-8c-64gb-1280s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring an AMD EPYC x86_64 processor with 8 vCPUs and 4 physical cores under a shared CPU allocation model. Operating at 2.0 GHz, the system is configured with 64.0 GB of memory and a bundled 1280 GB local NVMe SSD. Benchmark results show strong single-core CPU and memory performance, alongside average multi-core and database execution speeds. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core and large local storage capacity make this instance qualitatively cost-effective for memory-heavy workloads. However, the shared CPU allocation represents a tradeoff for sustained multi-core compute tasks. It is well-suited for database hosting, caching layers, and memory-intensive web serving.
og_description: Vultr voc-s-8c-64gb-1280s-amd is an AMD EPYC-powered server with 8 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 1280 GB NVMe SSD, offering strong single-core and memory performance for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Vultr voc-s-8c-64gb-1280s-amd features 8 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 1280 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong single-core and memory performance.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring high-capacity NVMe storage and strong single-core performance for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC architecture with 8 vCPUs and 4 physical cores
64.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Bundled 1280 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU and memory benchmark performance
Shared CPU allocation suited for bursty or memory-heavy tasks
Optimal for database hosting, caching, and memory-intensive applications
